mapping.a_element: compactification on a Z6-II orbifold to reach 4D
mapping.b_element: orbifold compactification of the 5D theory to reach 4D

mapping.a_element: SO(10) and E6 local GUT structures
mapping.b_element: SO(10) and E6 gauge groups / field content

mapping.a_element: string worldsheet selection rules (the 'gamma-rule') that decide which couplings and matter states are allowed
mapping.b_element: classification of homogeneous quaternionic scalar manifolds / supergravity constraints that decide which hypermultiplet couplings and matter content are allowed

brief_justification: Both build SO(10)/E6 orbifold-GUTs, but that is a shared design goal and vocabulary, not shared machinery: paper A's engine is a top-down heterotic string worldsheet construction whose spectrum and couplings come from stringy selection rules and D-flat decoupling, whereas paper B's engine is a bottom-up 5D N=2 Yang-Mills-Einstein supergravity in which the allowed matter/couplings are fixed by the geometric classification of quaternionic-Kahler scalar manifolds. The two mechanisms that actually select the matter content (string selection rules vs. quaternionic-manifold classification) have no role-for-role correspondence, so the underlying method differs.
